sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Innventure (INV) price target increased by 20.83% to 14.79

Despite the stock performance, analysts anticipate profitability this year, and the company recently reported strong Q4 results and announced new strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. $VZ Holdings Raised by Sigma Planning Corp

Verizon's Q1 earnings surpassed expectations and the company raised its FY26 profit guidance, but analysts note rising debt as a primary risk.

feed.title: The J M Smucker Q3 earnings on deck: Is a beat likely for SJM?
feed.summary: This article forecasts The J.M. Smucker Co.'s (SJM) upcoming Q3 earnings report, expected on February 28th before the bell. Analysts are anticipating revenue of $1.99 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.27, representing a decline from the prior year, primarily due to the divestiture of certain pet food brands. While the company has a mixed earnings surprise history, this quarter's outlook is cautiously optimistic based on sales growth in key segments and recent strategic moves.

feed.title: Deere names insider Brent Norwood as finance chief
feed.summary: Deere has appointed Brent Norwood, a company veteran of over two decades, as its new chief financial officer, effective May 1. Norwood previously served as vice president and finance director for Deere's Construction and Forestry division and John Deere Power Systems. He succeeds Joshua Jepsen, who departed in February, and takes over from interim finance chief Ryan Campbell.

feed.title: InflaRx to Report First Quarter 2026 Results on May 7,
feed.summary: InflaRx N.V. (Nasdaq: IFRX) has announced that it will publish its financial and operating results for the first quarter of 2026 on May 7, 2026, before the market opens. The company, which focuses on pioneering anti-inflammatory therapeutics, will not be holding a conference call for this report. InflaRx highlights its lead program, izicopan, and previously developed vilobelimab, both targeting the complement system.

feed.title: Chevron profit beats estimates on war-driven surge in oil prices
feed.summary: Chevron Corp. reported first-quarter profits that exceeded analyst estimates, driven by higher oil and natural gas prices and contributions from the Hess Corp. acquisition, despite production outages due to the Iran war. The company's adjusted net income was US$1.41 per share, significantly above the average analyst forecast. While Chevron repurchased US$2.5 billion in stock, CFO Eimear Bonner stated that more sustained price rallies would be needed to consider increasing buybacks.

feed.title: UBS cuts Clorox stock price target on weaker guidance outlook
feed.summary: UBS lowered its price target for Clorox Co. (NYSE:CLX) to $96 from $110, maintaining a Neutral rating, despite the company beating Q3 earnings expectations. The cut reflects concerns over Clorox's significantly weaker fiscal 2026 guidance, organic sales improvement taking longer than anticipated, and limited visibility on future earnings power. Other firms like Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Evercore ISI also lowered their price targets, citing similar issues such as reduced guidance, sales challenges, and margin pressures.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Lifts Price Target on Omnicom Group to $83 From $82, Keeps Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Omnicom Group (OMC) to $83 from $82, maintaining an "Equalweight" rating on the stock. The announcement comes amidst several recent analyst adjustments for Omnicom following what appears to be its Q1 2026 earnings, where the company reported increased non-GAAP net income and revenue, beating FactSet estimates.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Board Elects Two New Executive Vice Presidents, Declares Quarterly Dividend
authors: Sedef Salingan Sahin and Tapaswee Chandele
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company has elected Sedef Salingan Sahin as Chief Digital Officer and Tapaswee Chandele as Global Chief People Officer, both to executive vice president roles, underscoring the company's focus on digital transformation and workforce strategy. The board also declared a quarterly dividend of 53 cents per common share, payable July 1 to shareholders of record on June 15, reinforcing Coca-Cola's commitment to shareholder returns. These moves reflect the beverage giant's ongoing efforts to modernize operations and organizational capabilities while maintaining its reputation as a consistent dividend payer.

feed.title: Viatris presents data on low-dose contraceptive patch at ACOG
feed.summary: Viatris Inc. announced it will present data on its investigational low-dose estrogen combined hormonal contraceptive weekly patch at the 2026 ACOG Annual Clinical & Scientific Meeting. The presentations will cover contraceptive efficacy and safety from a Phase 3 study, adhesion profiles, pharmacokinetic data, and cycle control. The U.S. FDA has accepted the New Drug Application for the patch, with a target action date of July 30, 2026.

feed.title: Is Thermo Fisher’s New U.S. Bioprocess Design Center Shaping a Deeper Biopharma Strategy for TMO?
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has opened a new U.S. Bioprocess Design Center in Plainville, Massachusetts, to enhance its bioproduction offerings and assist customers in developing biologics. While this expansion reinforces Thermo Fisher's biopharma focus, the company's near-term narrative is more influenced by recent Q1 2026 results, showing sales of US$11,005 million and net income of US$1,651 million. The long-term financial impact of the center will take time to materialize, though it aims to strengthen Thermo Fisher's role as an end-to-end biopharma partner despite lingering risks from China weakness and tariff pressures.

feed.title: Industry Analysts Just Made An Upgrade To Their ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE) Revenue Forecasts
feed.summary: Analysts have significantly upgraded their near-term revenue forecasts for ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE), now expecting US$39 billion in 2026, a 9.8% increase from the previous year. Despite this revenue boost, the earnings per share forecast remains stable at US$5.71, and the price target of US$94.81 is reconfirmed, suggesting analysts don't anticipate this upgrade to drastically impact short-term valuation. The company's revenue growth is still projected to outperform the industry, even with an expected slowdown compared to its historical performance.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive’s (NYSE:CL) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) reported better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales increasing 8.4% year-on-year to $5.32 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The company also beat non-GAAP profit expectations, achieving $0.97 per share. This strong performance was driven by broad-based top and bottom-line growth across categories and divisions, with a balance of volume and pricing growth.

feed.title: KKR’s Privileged Legal Memos Uncovered in DOJ Antitrust Probe
feed.summary: A rare legal ruling has compelled KKR & Co. Inc. to surrender privileged communications with its lawyers to US antitrust authorities. The Department of Justice is investigating whether the private capital firm deliberately concealed information regarding deals from antitrust enforcers. This development was highlighted in Bloomberg's "Going Private" newsletter, emphasizing its unusual nature and potential implications for legal privilege in antitrust scrutiny.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive tops Q1 expectations after broad-based growth, shares gain
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) exceeded Q1 analyst expectations with adjusted EPS of $0.97 and revenue of $5.32 billion, driven by broad-based growth across all categories and most divisions. Despite a slight decrease in gross profit margin and GAAP EPS, the company's stock rose 1.9%, and it maintained its full-year 2026 guidance for net and organic sales growth. Colgate-Palmolive also announced an expansion of its Strategic Growth and Productivity Program.

feed.title: Weekly birth control patch data gets 6 slots at major OB-GYN meeting
feed.summary: Viatris announced that six abstracts on its investigational low-dose estrogen combined hormonal contraceptive (CHC) weekly patch will be presented at the 2026 American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG) Annual Clinical & Scientific Meeting. These presentations will include Phase 3 efficacy and safety results, as well as new data on patch adhesion and pharmacokinetics. The company also confirmed that the U.S. FDA has accepted its New Drug Application for the patch with a target action date of July 30, 2026.

feed.title: Textron awarded DIU contract to provide Tsunami USV to U.S. Navy
feed.summary: Textron Systems Corporation has been awarded a contract by the Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) to deliver multiple TSUNAMI Uncrewed Surface Vessels (USVs) for the U.S. Navy's Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) exercise and joint operations with SOUTHCOM and the U.S. Navy Fourth Fleet. The TSUNAMI USVs will demonstrate cooperative intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance, and targeting (ISR-T) capabilities, as well as manned-unmanned surface warfare. This initiative leverages Textron's expertise in autonomous systems and Brunswick Corporation's production capabilities to provide versatile, multi-mission uncrewed assets for the U.S. Navy.
